verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 encodes the type 1 IP3 receptor, a ligand-gated calcium channel resident in the endoplasmic reticulum membrane. Its canonical function is to release Ca2+ from ER lumenal stores upon IP3 binding; the receptor faces the cytoplasm/ER lumen, not the extracellular space. No TM domain topology exposes a substantial extracellular domain. No CD designation; no cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-death/oncogenic ecto-pool documented for ITPR1 specifically; (2) no tissue-restricted surface display; (3) not a lysosomal TM protein—no lysosomal exocytosis route; (4) no documented PM minority pool or secretory cycling to the plasma membrane outer face; (5) no covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner tethering. Literature evidence (PMIDs 23157623, 25069764) references ITPR1 only in calcium signaling/pathway context, not surface display. HGNC gene-group (IP3 receptors) is exclusively ER-resident. Verdict: endomembrane-resident.
